title: agrimonia procera f. glandulosa
intro: Agrimonia procera f. glandulosa, also known as sweet lueta, is a subspecies of the Agrimonia procera plant, which belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and stop bleeding. The health benefits of sweet lueta include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce menstrual cramps, and help to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular herbal remedy. Sweet lueta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
